evals.prompt: Create a simple landing page project with an index.html, a styles/main.css, and a scripts/app.js. Zip it up for me to download.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP file named landing-page.zip (or similar) where all files live inside a single root folder (e.g. landing-page/), not loose at the archive root.
expectations: The ZIP file contains exactly one top-level folder (e.g. landing-page/)
index.html is located at landing-page/index.html, not at the root of the ZIP
styles/main.css is at landing-page/styles/main.css
scripts/app.js is at landing-page/scripts/app.js
The skill used build_zip.py or an equivalent method that guarantees the root folder
verify_zip.py passes (or equivalent check confirms structure is correct)
The file is presented to the user via present_files

evals.prompt: Scaffold a Python Flask API project for me with app.py, requirements.txt, and a routes/ folder containing health.py and users.py. Give me the files.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP archive where all files live under a single named root folder (e.g. flask-api/), preserving the routes/ subdirectory.
expectations: The ZIP has exactly one top-level folder
app.py is inside the root folder, not at the archive root
routes/health.py and routes/users.py preserve the subdirectory structure inside the root folder
requirements.txt is inside the root folder
The root folder name is meaningful (e.g. flask-api/, not output/ or tmp/)
The ZIP is presented for download

evals.prompt: Package this for me — I need a downloadable ZIP of a React component library with src/Button.jsx, src/Modal.jsx, src/index.js, and a package.json.
evals.expected_output: A correctly structured ZIP where all files are nested inside one root folder matching the project name.
expectations: The ZIP contains exactly one root-level folder
src/Button.jsx, src/Modal.jsx, and src/index.js are all nested inside that root folder under src/
package.json is inside the root folder
No files appear loose at the archive root
The root folder name reflects the project (not generic names like 'files' or 'output')

evals.prompt: Create a ZIP of two separate mini-projects: a todo-app with index.html and app.js, and a calculator with index.html and calc.js. Bundle them together.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP where both projects are nested under a single root folder (e.g. projects/todo-app/ and projects/calculator/), not loose at the root.
expectations: The ZIP contains exactly one top-level root folder
todo-app/ and calculator/ are subdirectories inside that root folder
Each sub-project's files are inside their respective subdirectory
No files or folders appear loose at the archive root

evals.prompt: Write me three config files: .env.example, docker-compose.yml, and nginx.conf. Zip them and make them downloadable.
evals.expected_output: A ZIP with all three config files inside a single named root folder.
expectations: All three files (.env.example, docker-compose.yml, nginx.conf) are inside a root folder
No files are loose at the archive root
The .env.example file is included (dotfiles should not be excluded)
The ZIP is presented to the user for download

evals.prompt: I asked you to make me a project earlier and the ZIP you gave me has all files dumped at the root with no folder. Can you fix it?
evals.expected_output: Claude re-packages the project correctly with a single root folder and verifies the structure before presenting.
expectations: Claude acknowledges the structure problem
The re-packaged ZIP has exactly one top-level root folder
verify_zip.py (or equivalent check) is used to confirm the fix
The corrected ZIP is presented to the user

evals.prompt: Make me a Node.js CLI tool project. I want: bin/cli.js, lib/commands/init.js, lib/commands/run.js, lib/utils.js, package.json, and a README.md. Export as ZIP.
evals.expected_output: A deeply nested project ZIP with all directories preserved under one root folder.
expectations: The ZIP has exactly one top-level folder
bin/cli.js is at root-folder/bin/cli.js
lib/commands/init.js and lib/commands/run.js preserve the nested lib/commands/ structure
lib/utils.js, package.json, and README.md are all inside the root folder
No flattening of subdirectories has occurred
The ZIP is made available for download
